name: United Churches
description_short: United Churches Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union in Taylor, MI offering savings accounts, checking, auto loans, mortgages, personal loans, and credit cards.
description_long:
United Churches Credit Union is a federally insured credit union serving members in the Taylor, Michigan area. As a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution, it operates under the NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) regulatory framework rather than as a traditional bank.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts, youth accounts, certificates of deposit, checking accounts, auto loans, RV/camper loans, home equity loans, home improvement loans, mortgage loans, personal loans, education loans, and VISA credit cards. Members can access these services through online banking, mobile banking, 24/7 telephone banking, and in-person at their Taylor location. The credit union also provides ancillary services such as payroll deduction, direct deposit, wire transfers, money orders, VISA gift cards, and notary services.

United Churches distinguishes itself through the Diamond Member Rewards Program, which waives the $2 quarterly membership fee, provides free ATM/debit card replacement, and offers free notary service to qualified members. The program incentivizes member engagement by requiring use of three of five specified services (maintaining a loan, active checking account, using debit card 5+ times monthly, enrolling in direct deposit, or signing up for e-statements). Featured rates as of August 1, 2025 include new auto loans at 4.99% APR for 12-36 months, home equity at 9.00% APR, and home improvement loans at 7.49% APR.

As a credit union, United Churches prioritizes member benefits over profits and offers competitive rates compared to traditional banks. However, membership eligibility requirements and a limited physical footprint (single Taylor location) may restrict accessibility for some consumers. The organization's full governance structure and asset size are not disclosed on the website.
pros: Diamond Member Rewards Program waives $2 quarterly membership fee and provides free notary service and ATM/debit card replacement
Competitive auto loan rate of 4.99% APR for 12-36 months as of August 2025
24/7 telephone banking and mobile banking available alongside online services
Direct deposit and payroll deduction options available
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure means profits return to members rather than shareholders
Comprehensive lending products including auto, RV/camper, home equity, home improvement, personal, and education loans
cons: Single physical location in Taylor, MI limits branch access for members outside the immediate area
Membership eligibility requirements not clearly disclosed on website
Home equity loan rate of 9.00% APR is relatively high compared to typical market rates
No information provided about asset size, number of members, or institution history
Limited online presence and marketing information available
best_for: Michigan residents in or near Taylor seeking competitive auto and personal loan rates
Members who can meet Diamond Member Rewards Program requirements to waive fees
Homeowners seeking home equity or home improvement financing
Consumers preferring member-owned, not-for-profit financial institutions
diagnosis: United Churches Credit Union is best for Michigan residents near Taylor who qualify for membership and want competitive rates on auto, mortgage, and home loans with the benefit of a member-owned, not-for-profit structure. The main caveat is the single physical location and unclear membership eligibility criteria, which may limit accessibility and require online/phone banking for most transactions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
